commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the OpenAI speech provider to use the shared media voice-compatibility helper for response formats and adds OpenAI plus speech-core MP3 voice-note regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main preserves configured OpenAI mp3 responseFormat but reports voiceCompatible only for opus, and speech-core then leaves Telegram audioAsVoice unset for that voice-note path.
commands.review_followup: Contributor-supplied real behavior proof is required before merge; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to make on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(openai): mark mp3 TTS voice output compatible
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes OpenAI-compatible TTS voice-note compatibility for configured `responseFormat: "mp3"`.
- Reuses the shared media runtime voice-compatibility contract instead of hard-coding OpenAI response formats.
- Adds provider and speech-core regressions so provider-compatible MP3 output continues to route as Telegram voice delivery.

Fixes #80317

## Problem summary

The OpenAI speech provider only marked voice-note synthesis as voice-compatible when `responseFormat === "opus"`. That made OpenAI-compatible local TTS servers configured for MP3 produce an audio file result even though the shared media contract and Telegram send path already allow MP3 voice delivery.

## Root cause

`extensions/openai/speech-provider.ts` used a provider-local equality check for `opus`, while the rest of the TTS/channel path uses `isVoiceCompatibleAudio()` to decide whether a produced audio asset can be delivered as a voice note. Microsoft/Edge TTS already follows that shared helper path for MP3 output, so OpenAI-compatible MP3 output diverged from the existing runtime contract.

## Architectural reasoning

This keeps the behavior at the provider boundary: the OpenAI provider still reports compatibility only for a `voice-note` synthesis target, but it now asks the shared media runtime whether the selected container extension is voice-compatible. The change does not alter provider selection, gateway/session flow, async cleanup, request timing, or Telegram transport behavior. It only corrects the metadata emitted by OpenAI-compatible synthesis results so the existing speech-core and Telegram delivery contracts can do what they already support.

## Testing performed

Local proof on the final rebased head `52a50a8e048ac84c6e515fd464f42feb9f78e931`:

- `pnpm test extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
  - `extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts`: 10 passed
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`: 28 passed
  - `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`: 99 passed
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/openai/speech-provider.ts extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/openai/speech-provider.ts extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
- `.
ode_modules\.bin\tsgo.cmd -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `.
ode_modules\.bin\tsgo.cmd -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `.
ode_modules\.bin\tsgo.cmd -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true`
- `pnpm check:changed` exited 0 in this checkout before the final rebase; the final rebased head was then covered by the focused local tests above and the full PR CI below.

GitHub Actions on the final head:

- CI run `206936`: success
- Workflow Sanity run `201233`: success
- OpenGrep PR Diff run `20924`: success
- CodeQL Critical Quality run `16898`: success

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI-compatible TTS configured with `responseFormat: "mp3"` should mark voice-note synthesis output as voice-compatible so Telegram delivery can use the voice path instead of falling back to audio-file delivery.

Real environment tested: Local Windows checkout on branch `Hemant/fix-openai-tts-mp3-voice`, rebased onto current `upstream/main`, Node `v22.15.0` with pnpm running under the repository install. The proof uses production OpenAI provider logic, speech-core routing logic, and Telegram send tests with mocked Telegram transport.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused Vitest command covering the OpenAI provider, speech-core TTS routing, and Telegram send behavior: `pnpm test extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`.

Evidence after fix: The OpenAI provider regression asserts MP3 voice-note synthesis returns `outputFormat: "mp3"`, a `.mp3` file extension, and `voiceCompatible === true`. The speech-core regression asserts provider-compatible MP3 voice-note synthesis sets Telegram media delivery to `audioAsVoice: true`. The existing Telegram send regression confirms MP3 with `audioAsVoice` uses `sendVoice`.

Observed result after fix: All three focused test files passed after the final rebase: 10 OpenAI provider tests, 28 speech-core tests, and 99 Telegram send tests. Formatting, diff whitespace, focused linting, focused type checks, and all GitHub PR workflows passed.

What was not tested: I did not send a live Telegram message or call a live OpenAI-compatible TTS server with real credentials. `pnpm build` was also not accepted end-to-end on this Windows path because `scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` fails to quote a workspace path containing a space during `build:plugin-sdk:dts`; the exact failing `tsgo` command passed when invoked directly through the quoted local binary, and the GitHub `build-artifacts` job passed on Linux.

## Risk analysis

- Runtime behavior: Narrow metadata correction for OpenAI-compatible TTS results only.
- Concurrency/session/gateway flows: No changes.
- Cleanup/lifecycle handling: No changes.
- API behavior: No public config or SDK shape changes.
- Backward compatibility: Existing Opus voice-note behavior stays compatible; WAV remains non-voice-compatible through the shared helper.
- Developer ergonomics: Removes duplicated provider-local format policy and aligns OpenAI with the shared media contract already used by other providers.

## Backward compatibility

Backward compatible. Users who explicitly configured `responseFormat: "mp3"` for OpenAI-compatible TTS now get the expected voice-compatible metadata for voice-note targets. Non-voice targets and formats that are not accepted by the shared media helper keep their previous fallback behavior.
target.author: hemantsudarshan
